calendar.matters:
REQUEST FOR ORDER OF CHILD SUPPORT Modification Of Stop Enforcement Of Collection On Arrearages Or Reduce Monthly Liquidation Amount
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: (416) 08/20/2014
(Wightman) Clerk: K. M. Lee; Court Reporter: Lewis Gillian, CSR #12355; Bailiff: Deputy Henry. General advisement given in regards to Commissioner Rebecca Wightman sitting as a judge pro tempore. Mother not present; Respondent (Father) present with a support person, Michael Fuller; DCSS representative, Erica Prado, present; CSA = Boselli. Matter is on calendar for hearing on Request for Order re: Modification - Stop Enforcement of Collection on Arrearages or Reduce Monthly Liquidation Amount; Request for Hearing re: Earnings Assignment. Oral statements are made in open court. Father alleges he is receiving benefits which is SSI and provides to DCSS attorney a letter from Social Security in open court. DCSS attorney reviews the document then provides it to the court for review. Court and DCSS determines father is receiving social security disability benefits, not SSI benefits. Court partially grants the Request for Order such that the court reduces the liquidation amount with the garnishment to be adjusted accordingly. In addition, the court enters an order for the immediate release of the hold placed by DCSS on father's licenses pending further order. This release is without prejudice to DCSS seeking to re-suspend through the filing of a motion or ex parte application. Court recommends father first speak with DCSS directly if he encounters any issues with the collections like he has experienced. Court reserves jurisdiction for it standard 90 days following formal service of the order. Court recommends father deal with IRS if he has any issues concerning the garnishment by the Dept. of the Treasury. The Findings and Orders of the Court were signed and filed in court this day, and are incorporated herein by reference.

Request For Hearing Re: Earnings Assignment
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order

The Findings and Orders of the Court were signed and filed in court this day, and are incorporated herein by reference.
